match: CSK vs MI — IPL 2026 (example demo, Chepauk)

insights.scout: Ruturaj Gaikwad anchors a settled top order; CSK prefers spin-friendly middle overs at Chepauk.
insights.stats: CSK–MI head-to-head at this venue slightly favours the home side on average first-innings totals.
insights.weather: Warm humid evening; negligible rain risk; dew possible in the second innings.
insights.pitch: Traditional Chepauk slow-down — cutters and spinners get purchase after the powerplay.
insights.news: Both camps report full-strength squads; toss and first six overs remain the main tactical hinge.
insights.live: Demo snapshot — paste a live line from the command bar when you run a real war room.

debateRounds.text: Home control, spin depth, and a bank of finishers give CSK the structural edge.
MI can race away in the powerplay; if they neutralise the middle-overs squeeze, CSK's plan thins.
Pathirana and Theekshana in tandem tighten death overs; chasing under lights is harder than MI think.
Bumrah and a power-hitting middle order can break the Chepauk handbrake if the ball swings early.
Crowd lift and micro-matchups in the middle tilt marginal moments to the yellow camp.
One explosive over from a finisher flips pressure; MI only need thin margins to steal a tight game.

verdict.summary: Both sides land credible cases; Judge leans CSK on venue control and late spin squeeze versus MI's powerplay upside.
